branches.condition: Developer chooses a messaging channel that does not require SDK (e.g., email or SMS)
branches.effect_on_first_success: Faster onboarding by configuring channel without code integration

branches.condition: Developer enables identity verification for user login
branches.effect_on_first_success: Improved security but added setup complexity
branches.path: Additional configuration and server-side JWT setup

candidate_paths.name: API-only onboarding path
candidate_paths.relationship_to_primary_path: Alternative to Dashboard UI message sending; requires prior app and channel setup
candidate_paths.selection_basis: For developers who prefer to use REST API directly after app and channel setup

details: Same as primary path step 1 and 2
steps.output: App with App ID and API keys

details: Construct API request with App ID and API key
Send POST request to messages endpoint
steps.output: API response indicating success or failure

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Send messages — compose campaigns, personalize content, and build automated Journeys

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Message sent and received by at least one user subscription
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Message delivery confirmation in OneSignal Dashboard or API response indicating successful send
documented_first_success.official_milestone: Successfully send a message through a configured channel from the OneSignal Dashboard or API
documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The documentation defines sending a message as the first major milestone after setup, marking the platform's core functionality being operational.

assumed_prior_state: Developer has internet access and a device to access the OneSignal Dashboard
Developer has an existing app or website to integrate messaging
entry_point.developer_intent: Create a OneSignal account and app to send notifications via a chosen messaging channel.

friction_gates.description: Creating a OneSignal account is required to access the Dashboard and create apps.
Configuring at least one messaging channel is required before sending messages.
SDK integration is required for push and in-app messaging channels.
API keys must be secured and used properly to authenticate API requests.

prerequisites.requirement: Create a OneSignal account or be invited to an existing Organization
Create a OneSignal App within the Organization to hold user and messaging data
Choose and configure at least one messaging channel (e.g., mobile push, web push, email, SMS)
Integrate the OneSignal SDK for channels requiring SDK (mobile push, web push, in-app messages)
Obtain and secure API keys and App ID for authentication and API usage

primary_path.action: Create a OneSignal account or log in
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Login error or account creation failure
primary_path.input: Email and password or third-party login

primary_path.action: Create a new OneSignal App in the Dashboard
primary_path.output: New app created with unique App ID
primary_path.success_signal: App visible in Dashboard with App ID

primary_path.action: Choose and configure at least one messaging channel
details: For push: follow SDK installation and platform-specific setup
For email/SMS: configure sender domains or phone numbers
Set up credentials and permissions as required
primary_path.output: Channel enabled and configured in the app
primary_path.success_signal: Channel status shows as active or configured

primary_path.action: Integrate OneSignal SDK into app code for channels requiring SDK
details: Implement user subscription and permission prompts
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Build errors or SDK initialization failures
primary_path.input: SDK installation commands and App ID
primary_path.output: SDK integrated and initialized in app

primary_path.action: Send a test message or campaign from the Dashboard or API
details: Select target audience or test users

sections_used: Set up your account and messaging channels
sources.title: Getting started with OneSignal

alternatives_considered.name: REST API only
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The REST API requires prior app and channel setup in the Dashboard and is used after initial onboarding steps.

alternatives_considered.name: Third-party integrations
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Integrations are for extending functionality after initial setup, not for first-mile onboarding.

surface.name: Dashboard and SDK
surface.selection_basis: The official documentation emphasizes starting from the OneSignal Dashboard to create an app and configure messaging channels, then integrating the SDK for push and in-app messages or configuring email/SMS channels without SDK. This is the primary developer surface for onboarding.

uncertainties.impact: Without a vendor claim or explicit documentation, time estimates for onboarding duration remain unclear, affecting developer expectations.
uncertainties.question: What is the typical or average time to first success (sending a message) claimed or expected by OneSignal?
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: No explicit time-to-first-success or onboarding duration claim was found in the official documentation sources.
